action.reasoning: Observation: the last command mostly succeeded in loading and running inference; the only immediate failure was incorrect score_val.py usage. More importantly, eval portability still hinges on model.py path resolution, and current workspace code is truncated so safest is to rewrite model.py/predict.py/score_val.py cleanly and smoke-test exactly like eval with an absolute checkpoint path. Analysis: we do not need more training yet. The highest-value step is to make inference self-contained and robust for the existing best LoRA checkpoint, verify local predict end-to-end on val, and then submit eval. Decision: replace model.py with a compact inference-focused implementation that searches multiple relative packaged base-model locations, loads processor/model offline, robustly parses outputs, clips coordinates, and always emits a point fallback. Also fix score_val.py CLI and run local smoke test using absolute checkpoint path before submitting eval.
